behaviors.description: Storing a PASS review summary is blocked when any stored dimension finding for the same round has a FAIL verdict.
behaviors.justification: Candidate tests: Unit can verify a pure `canStorePass(findings)` function, but misses cross-module bugs in round-scoped query/filtering and stale repository reads. Integration wires summary write path + findings store and catches real failures where FAIL exists in storage but PASS still persists. Higher levels rejected: behavior text is about stored verdict consistency, not real OS/network/subprocess, not model output quality, not multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Create findings for one round with at least one FAIL in storage, then attempt to persist a PASS summary through the service/repository boundary; assert write is rejected and includes the blocking FAIL dimension context.

behaviors.description: Stop-gate verdict computation reads per-dimension stored findings directly and does not rely only on hand-written summary text.
behaviors.justification: Candidate tests: Unit can check a verdict reducer given findings input, but misses orchestration defects where gate code accidentally reads summary text field. Integration with summary store + findings store catches the real boundary bug: summary says PASS while stored dimensions include FAIL. Higher levels rejected: no behavior text requiring real infra or AI output; this is deterministic data-source selection across modules.
behaviors.test_description: Persist contradictory data (summary text says all PASS; stored per-dimension findings contain FAIL) and run stop-gate computation through production module wiring; assert verdict is FAIL/blocking based on stored findings.

behaviors.description: Manual summary text overrides require an explicit override flag and produce an auditable warning record.
behaviors.justification: Candidate tests: Unit can validate flag-check logic, but misses failure where audit logger/repository is not invoked or transaction ordering drops warning records. Integration catches flag enforcement plus audit side effect across modules. Higher levels rejected: behavior text does not require OS/network/subprocess or model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke summary update without override flag and assert rejection; invoke with override flag and assert success plus persisted audit warning record containing actor, round, timestamp, and override reason metadata.

behaviors.description: After Phase 4 fixes, the gate requires a review round identifier produced after the latest fix commit before PASS can be finalized.
behaviors.justification: Candidate tests: Unit can compare `roundCommit > latestFixCommit`, but misses integration failures in fetching latest fix commit and binding it to the round metadata used by gate finalization. Integration catches stale linkage and wrong commit-source wiring. Higher level rejection: System considered for real git/subprocess timing, but behavior text only requires identifier ordering/association; deterministic persisted commit metadata can reproduce failure without real git process.
behaviors.test_description: Store fix commit metadata and an older review-round id, attempt PASS finalization and assert block; then store a newer post-fix review-round id and assert PASS can proceed.

behaviors.description: In the live review loop, PASS cannot finalize unless a fresh review-agent round runs on the updated diff.
behaviors.justification: Candidate tests: Agentic single-step can verify one model call occurs, but misses sequence bug where fix commit happens and gate still finalizes using pre-fix round. Workflow test (fix -> updated diff -> new review-agent round -> gate decision) catches missing handoff freshness across steps. Lower Integration misses real loop sequencing; stubs can falsely pass by injecting synthetic round ids without proving the live pipeline reran. Higher level not needed beyond Workflow because failure boundary is this multi-step agent pipeline itself.
behaviors.test_description: Run a multi-step live loop: create failing review round, apply fix commit that changes diff, attempt PASS without rerun (must fail), then execute a new review-agent round on updated diff and verify PASS is only eligible after that fresh round is recorded.

behaviors.description: If review rounds are exhausted with unresolved MUST-FIX findings in stored results, the outcome is escalation rather than PASS.
behaviors.justification: Candidate tests: Unit can check a decision table `(roundsExhausted && unresolvedMustFix) => escalate`, but misses repository/state aggregation defects (wrong unresolved-count query, wrong round-limit source). Integration catches real failure at state handoff boundary between findings store and gate policy module. Higher levels rejected: behavior text is about stored results and policy outcome, not real infra or model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Seed stored results with unresolved MUST-FIX findings and round counter at configured max; execute gate outcome computation through service layer and assert escalation status plus PASS prohibition.

behaviors.description: In a live multi-round review workflow, exhausted rounds with unresolved MUST-FIX findings still block PASS and emit escalation.
behaviors.justification: Candidate tests: Integration can simulate final stored state and assert escalate, but misses live orchestration bugs across repeated rounds (carryover of MUST-FIX state, exhaustion counter updates, final gate emission timing). Workflow exercises multi-round sequence and catches failures only visible when state transitions occur over several agent rounds. Rejected lower levels because behavior text explicitly requires 'live multi-round review workflow'.
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end multi-round loop until max rounds is reached while preserving unresolved MUST-FIX findings; verify finalization blocks PASS and emits escalation event/record at exhaustion boundary.
